title: HTTP Security Headers Auditor & Security Scorer
description: An automated domain scanner that evaluates server response configurations for modern security headers (CSP, HSTS, CORS) and outputs structured compliance scores.
markdown:
### 📋 Project Overview

The **HTTP Security Headers Auditor** is a command-line script that analyzes HTTP response configurations against industry security baselines (such as OWASP and Mozilla Observatory). It evaluates key protective headers and generates security compliance reports in JSON, Markdown, or HTML formats.

---

### 🛡️ Key Auditing Features

- **Passive Headers Assessment**:
  Checks domains for the presence and configuration of:
  - `Content-Security-Policy` (CSP)
  - `Strict-Transport-Security` (HSTS)
  - `X-Frame-Options` (Clickjacking defense)
  - `X-Content-Type-Options` (MIME sniffing defense)
  - `Referrer-Policy` & `Permissions-Policy`
- **Dynamic Scoring Engine**:
  Assigns a numeric score (0 to 100) based on header properties, penalizing weak parameters (like unsafe CSP policies) or missing protections.
- **Server Information Leak Check**:
  Flags headers that expose underlying software and version structures (e.g., `Server`, `X-Powered-By`).

---

### 🚀 CLI Usage & Configuration

Run the auditor against any domain:

```bash
# Audit a single website
python main.py -d https://target.com

# Save structured report as JSON
python main.py -d https://target.com -o report.json --format json
```

---

### 🧠 Core Security Concepts

HTTP security headers act as an essential first line of defense for browsers. For example:
- **`Content-Security-Policy` (CSP)**: Defines trusted sources of executable scripts, preventing cross-site scripting (XSS).
- **`Strict-Transport-Security` (HSTS)**: Forces the browser to connect exclusively via secure HTTPS channels, mitigating SSL stripping and man-in-the-middle (MitM) attacks.

---

### 🛡️ Remediation Guidelines

Configure your web servers (Nginx, Apache, or Cloudflare rules) to inject these headers:

```nginx
# Recommended Nginx configuration
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://trustedscripts.com;" always;
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
```

---

### 🔗 Project Source & Reference

You can review the full open-source implementation, contribute pull requests, and view the code structure on GitHub:
👉 [GitHub Repository: http-headers-auditor](https://github.com/amooryx/http-headers-auditor)
